title: Set stable relay identity key and enable REST token auth
description: relayEnv sets neither BUZZ_RELAY_PRIVATE_KEY nor BUZZ_REQUIRE_AUTH_TOKEN. The relay binary then falls back to a hardcoded dev keypair (secp256k1 privkey 1, identical on every deployment) and serves REST endpoints with token auth bypassed, on the only public interface (:3000). Generate a 32-byte hex relay key in seedFiles, add to store.json schema, set both env vars. Binary enforces BUZZ_RELAY_PRIVATE_KEY when BUZZ_REQUIRE_AUTH_TOKEN=true, so both must land together.

title: Changing s3Bucket orphans all previously uploaded media
description: The config action allows s3Bucket to be edited at any time. On next start minio-init creates the new bucket (mc mb --ignore-existing) and relayEnv points BUZZ_S3_BUCKET at it, leaving every previously uploaded object stranded in the old bucket with no migration path. The only warning is the generic 'Changes take effect the next time Buzz Relay starts.' Either make s3Bucket install-time-only, or warn explicitly that existing media becomes unreachable. See startos/actions/config.ts and startos/main.ts relayEnv.

title: Relay :3000 interface + clearnet TLS for buzz.privkey.io
description: Buzz Desktop needs publicly-trusted wss cert. Prefer StartOS clearnet -> buzz.privkey.io. Alt: VPS Caddy. Health check :8080/_readiness.

title: Build .s9pk, install on box, e2e test in Buzz Desktop
description: start-sdk build -> install -> add wss://buzz.privkey.io as community; verify channels/DMs/media(MinIO/Blossom)/git; confirm backup/restore survives data.

title: Config spec + secret generation
description: Auto-gen POSTGRES_PASSWORD, REDIS_PASSWORD, BUZZ_S3_ACCESS_KEY/SECRET. Expose bucket name (buzz-media) and BUZZ_AUTO_MIGRATE (default true).

title: Write manifest: metadata, versions, volumes, interfaces, backups
description: Model on ../synapse-startos. Volumes: postgres data, redis AOF, minio data, /data/git.

title: Scaffold package from hello-world-startos (TS SDK)
description: Copy structure from ../hello-world-startos. Package id buzz-relay. RESOLVED: box=StartOS 0.4.0-beta.9, SDK=@start9labs/start-sdk 2.0.6. All 3 reference repos already target 0.4.x (osVersions 0.4.0-0.4.3) — use as-is, no branch switch.
close_reason: Scaffolded from hello-world-startos: copied startos/ tree, build files (package.json, Makefile, tsconfig, .dockerignore, icon.svg, LICENSE), .github/workflows, assets/. Set package id=buzz-relay, title=Buzz Relay, license=Apache-2.0, upstream=block/buzz, image=ghcr.io/block/buzz:main (x86_64+aarch64). Rebranded i18n/versions/main/interfaces. Verified: tsc --noEmit exit 0, ncc bundle build exit 0. NOTE: LICENSE file still MIT text (wrapper license TBD); daemon exec + manifest volumes/interfaces are placeholders owned by 67x/63j.

title: Package buzz-relay as a StartOS .s9pk
description: Wrap Block's buzz-relay (Buzz Community backend) as a StartOS package so a self-hosted community runs on the amd64 Start9 box and can be added in Buzz Desktop at wss://buzz.privkey.io. Full plan in PLAN.md. Image ghcr.io/block/buzz:main (multi-arch). Stack: relay + postgres:17 + redis:7 + minio + minio-init.

title: Implement 4 daemons + minio-init with health-gated ordering
description: postgres healthy -> redis healthy -> minio healthy -> minio-init completes -> relay. Model on synapse Postgres bundling.
close_reason: Implemented postgres/redis/minio/relay daemons + minio-init and prepare-data oneshots with health-gated ordering in startos/main.ts

title: Map env vars; rewire service hostnames to localhost
description: compose uses postgres/redis/minio DNS; in-package daemons share localhost -> 127.0.0.1:5432/6379/9000. Set BUZZ_AUTO_MIGRATE=true.
close_reason: env vars mapped to 127.0.0.1 + BUZZ_AUTO_MIGRATE wired in main.ts
